expected_main_papers.title: VisualBERT: A Simple and Performant Baseline for Vision and Language
expected_main_papers.note: Provides a baseline for joint image-region and text-token transformer pretraining.

expected_main_papers.title: SimVLM: Simple Visual Language Model Pretraining with Weak Supervision
expected_main_papers.note: Uses a simple prefix language modeling objective for weakly supervised VLM pretraining.

expected_main_papers.title: Multimodal Few-Shot Learning with Frozen Language Models
expected_main_papers.note: Shows how image embeddings can condition a frozen language model.

expected_main_papers.title: ClipCap: CLIP Prefix for Image Captioning
expected_main_papers.note: Maps CLIP image embeddings into a language-model prefix for captioning.

expected_main_papers.title: Flamingo: a Visual Language Model for Few-Shot Learning
expected_main_papers.note: Uses gated cross-attention to inject visual features into a language model.

expected_main_papers.title: CoCa: Contrastive Captioners are Image-Text Foundation Models
expected_main_papers.note: Combines contrastive image-text learning with caption generation.

expected_main_papers.title: An Empirical Study of GPT-3 for Few-Shot Knowledge-Based VQA
expected_main_papers.note: Uses prompting and GPT-3 for knowledge-based visual question answering.

expected_main_papers.title: Socratic Models: Composing Zero-Shot Multimodal Reasoning with Language
expected_main_papers.note: Composes vision and language models through language as a multimodal interface.

expected_concept_sections.name: 1. Why combine vision and language
required_ideas: motivate connecting visual perception with language reasoning
explain the central question of how image representations enter a language model
set up tradeoffs among joint fusion, prefixing, cross-attention, contrastive-captioning, and tool composition

expected_concept_sections.name: 2. Joint image-text pretraining
required_ideas: jointly model image regions/features and text tokens
VisualBERT as an early joint transformer baseline
SimVLM as weakly supervised simple visual-language pretraining

expected_concept_sections.name: 3. Image embeddings as language-model prefixes
required_ideas: map image embeddings into LM prefix/prompt space
freeze or lightly train the language model
Frozen and ClipCap as prefix-conditioning examples

expected_concept_sections.name: 4. Cross-attention fusion
required_ideas: inject visual features through cross-attention layers
Flamingo-style gated cross-attention over visual tokens
support few-shot multimodal learning

expected_concept_sections.name: 5. Contrastive captioner foundation models
required_ideas: combine contrastive image-text learning with captioning
support retrieval and generation in a single model family
CoCa as contrastive-captioning foundation model

expected_concept_sections.name: 6. No-training and tool-composition approaches
required_ideas: use prompting or external model composition instead of retraining a monolithic model
PICa/GPT-3-style few-shot VQA
Socratic Models compose vision and language modules through language

expected_concept_sections.name: 7. Practical synthesis
required_ideas: summarize where visual information enters the language model
compare joint fusion, prefix, cross-attention, contrastive-captioning, and tool composition
explain tradeoffs in training cost, flexibility, and reasoning ability

quality_checks: HTML opens in a local HTTP server with visible styling from stylesheet.min.css
LaTeX formulas render through MathJax rather than appearing as raw TeX when formulas are present
Blog is image-rich and every included figure has a visible source-paper attribution
The blog explains paper selection and section-paper mapping in selection_note.md
The final result directory includes the HTML and all referenced assets needed for offline viewing
The blog should be a coherent paper-reading article, not just a bullet list or copied abstracts
All HTML-referenced assets resolve relative to the delivered HTML path and return 200 under a local HTTP server
